title: Alon–Stiebitz Digraph Splitting Problem (Problem 1)
statement_text: Does there exist, for all $s, t \geq 1$, a number $F(s,t) \in \mathbb{N}$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq F(s,t)$ has a partition $V(D) = A \sqcup B$ such that $\delta^+(D[A]) \geq s$ and $\delta^+(D[B]) \geq t$?
context_text: This is the main open problem in the area of digraph splitting with minimum out-degree constraints, posed independently by Stiebitz in 1995 and by Alon in 2006. The only known finite case prior to this paper is $F(1,1) < \infty$, which is equivalent to the existence of two disjoint directed cycles in any digraph of sufficiently large minimum out-degree; whether $F(2,2) < \infty$ or even $F(1,2) < \infty$ remained open. An entry for this problem exists on the Open Problem Garden.
paper_contribution: Proves that if $F(2,2) < \infty$ then $F(s,t) = \Theta(s+t)$ for all $s, t \geq 1$ (and analogously $F(s,1) = \Theta(s)$ if $F(2,1) < \infty$), thereby reducing the entire problem to deciding whether $F(2,2) < \infty$.
